title: LB Test Frame MV3 Extension — manifest architecture
insight: manifest.json uses MV3 (manifest_version:3) with minimal permissions: storage+tabs+activeTab+localhost:7712. Content scripts run at document_idle on the five named AI vendor domains only. Options page and web-accessible onboarding+verify pages declared. Permissions justification doc in CHROME_STORE_SUBMISSION.md.

title: Background service worker — persona state + message router
insight: background.js extends K485A with full three-persona state management. DEFAULT_PREFS seeds all keys. Message router handles: ENRICH_QUERY (cathedral injection), PING_DAEMON, GET_PREFS, SET_PREF, SET_PREFS, DETECT_AI_SESSIONS, SUBMIT_VERIFY_RESULTS, OPEN_VERIFY_TAB. Telemetry submission is gated: only called on non-private share preferences.

title: Content script — cathedral injection with explicit member consent
insight: content.js intercepts keydown Enter events on AI vendor pages. Member sees an overlay with 'Send original' vs 'Send with LB context' before any submission happens. GUARDRAIL: never auto-types; member chooses per-query. 15-second auto-dismiss sends original (safe default). Overlay uses DOM-native value setter + React-style input events to work with AI vendor textarea React state.

title: AI session detection — best-effort DOM heuristics
insight: detectAISessions() queries all open tabs, matches URL patterns against five vendor regexes. likelyLoggedIn is inferred from tab title (absence of 'sign in'). Result is presented tentatively to member per Phase B.1 spec — no claim of certainty. Returns vendor map with tabCount, signupUrl, likelyLoggedIn.

title: Three-step onboarding flow — Persona Picker → Pick Your AI → Ready
insight: onboarding.html + onboarding.js implement the three-step first-launch flow. Step 1: four persona cards (Casual, Developer, Member, All-three). Step 2: renderAIVendors() shows detected sessions first; showAllAIs() reveals all five vendors. Step 3: setup-ready confirmation with two CTAs (skip verification, run demo). All state written via SET_PREFS on finishOnboarding.

title: LRH dialog moments — centralized for Founder rewriting
insight: platform/src/lib/lrh/dialog_moments.ts exports LRH_MOMENTS record with six named entries (first_launch, persona_picker, pick_your_ai, demo_start, demo_results, membership_welcome). Each has id, surface, text, assetKey. interpolateLRH() handles runtime template vars like [X] for improvement count. Founder can rewrite text without touching any component code.

title: Three-persona definitions — Casual / Developer / Member / All-three
insight: Persona picker exposes four options. 'All-three' installs all modules; persona is a setting, not a distribution. Casual = no API keys, cathedral injection + demo. Developer = API key management + R13/R14 harness CLI. Member = $5/yr → Helm transformation. Switching is in-place: no re-installation, no data loss.

title: 25-question verification battery — bundled fallback bank
insight: shared/question-bank/fallback_bank.json contains 25 Founder-approved questions covering LB economics, platform mechanics, technology (Romulator, Slow Blade, Furnace, Cathedral Effect), identity, IP facts, and governance. Questions test knowledge only available in the LB cathedral — cold AI sessions consistently miss them. bank_id: lb-fallback-v1, version: 1.0.0.

title: Verification demo flow — cold phase → cathedral phase → grade
insight: verify.js implements the per-question two-phase flow: cold-ask (member pastes unprimed AI answer) → submitColdAnswer() grades + reveals cathedral-card with context block → cathedral-ask (member pastes primed answer) → submitCathedralAnswer() grades + advances. Skip functions for partial completion. gradeAnswer() uses case-insensitive substring matching against correct_answers array.

title: Cathedral context templates — per-question-category pre-injection paragraphs
insight: CONTEXT_TEMPLATES in verify.js supplies a different cathedral paragraph per question category (economics, platform, technology, identity, ip, governance, research). Member pastes the template text into their AI before asking the question. This is the manual-injection path — no automated DOM typing; member remains in full control.

title: Results display — lift calculation + LRH result text + comparison to R13
insight: showResults() computes coldPct, cathedralPct, liftPp. LRH result text varies by lift direction. Comparison sentence explicitly handles low-end results honestly ('this can happen because of variations…') per Guardrail — never spins low lift as architecture failure. PUBLISHED_MEAN_LIFT = 86 pp (R13). Partial-run flagged as fewer-significance-bars via questionsCompleted check.

title: opt_in_share flow — three-level consent gate
insight: shareResult() in verify.js calls SET_PREF to persist share_preference, then SUBMIT_VERIFY_RESULTS. Background's submitVerifyResults() returns immediately without network call if preference='private'. Anonymous shares strip member_id before POST. Public shares include member_id. Member sees distinct feedback per choice. Right-to-deletion endpoint /api/test_frame_results/delete?member_id= provided.

title: test_frame_results Supabase table + aggregate view
insight: Migration 20260425130001 creates test_frame_results with cold/cathedral/lift columns, share_preference CHECK constraint, member_id nullable (only non-null for public shares). Aggregate view test_frame_results_aggregate computes mean/median/range lift + by-vendor breakdown using PERCENTILE_CONT. RLS: public reads on anonymous+public rows; service-role writes; member deletes own.

title: test-frame-results edge function — POST/GET/DELETE routing
insight: platform/supabase/functions/test-frame-results/index.ts handles three routes: POST (insert with privacy enforcement), GET /aggregate (read aggregate view), DELETE /delete?member_id= (right-to-deletion). CORS headers allow extension origin. Validates required fields; clamps hot_pct to 0-100. Service-role key used server-side only.

title: Community Empirical Dashboard — public aggregate page
insight: platform/src/pages/CommunityEmpiricalDashboard.tsx at librarian.the2ndsecond.com/community-empirical. Fetches aggregate from edge function every 5 minutes. Zero-state shows CTA to install. Non-zero state shows headline claim, 4-metric grid (runs, mean_lift_pp, cold_pct, cathedral_pct), by-vendor lift bars, methodology note. AAAI §6 load-bearing claim surface.

title: Distribution landing page — lb-test-frame.lianabanyan.com
insight: platform/src/pages/TestFrameLanding.tsx serves the member-facing download page. OS download buttons (Mac DMG, Windows MSI, Linux DEB/RPM, Chrome extension). Three-mode breakdown (Casual/Developer/Member). Six-item FAQ (API key? cost? privacy? AI support? Cathedral Effect? result sharing?). All download URLs in DOWNLOAD_LINKS constant for easy update post-CWS-approval.

title: Electron packaging — signed DMG + MSI + DEB/RPM
insight: lb-test-frame/electron/electron-builder.yml configures: Mac (x64+arm64 DMG, notarize=true, hardened runtime); Windows (x64 MSI+NSIS, signingHashAlgorithms sha256); Linux (x64+arm64 DEB+RPM+AppImage). Bundled question bank via extraFiles. Publish to GitHub Releases. Signing env vars: APPLE_ID/APPLE_TEAM_ID for Mac; WINDOWS_CERT_FILE for Windows.

title: Chrome Web Store submission ZIP build script
insight: build-extension-zip.mjs uses archiver to create lb-test-frame-extension.zip from extension/ + shared/ directories. Excludes CHROME_STORE_SUBMISSION.md and dev artifacts. Outputs to dist/. Run via npm run make:extension-zip. Submission checklist in CHROME_STORE_SUBMISSION.md covers: permissions justification, screenshots (1280×800), promo tile (440×280), privacy policy URL, icon requirements.
